(1 comment)

Hmm. I don't know if this is complete. A few pieces are missing but I don't know how it works for earlier kernels. The kernel commits of interest are:

b788dc51e4255b700f566a5d63a9c5028d631c77

6a42e615a28bad49f2e04829486e94190c066390

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

Mark this -1 until some things are clarified.

Patch Set 7: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 7:

James,
I fixed issue you refering into commits: b788dc51e4255b700f566a5d63a9c5028d631c77
6a42e615a28bad49f2e04829486e94190c066390
in other way. I just free all cached acls for inode just before clear it (llite_lib.c:1398). This allow to use advantage of cached acls and correctly free them before free. Your solution don't allow to cache acls.

parents.subject: LU-9183 llite: handle xattr with the xattr_handler infrastructure

commit.subject: LU-9183 llite: add support set_acl method in inode operations
commit.message:
LU-9183 llite: add support set_acl method in inode operations

Linux kernel v3.14 adds set_acl method to inode operations.
See kernel commit 893d46e443346370cd4ea81d9d35f72952c62a37

Change-Id: Ia40d55364016fafa8633fdaecd317910505f8ad4
Signed-off-by: Dmitry Eremin <dmitry.eremin@intel.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/25965
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
